test.name: Gate proceeds past corroborating-only unlinked assertions
test.description: Regression test for the narrowed precondition gate. In flynn-parentage-disproved, two assertions — a_002 (Patrick's ~1845 birth derived from the 1850 census age) and a_006 (the same birth year from the 1908 death-certificate age) — have no person_evidence link, but both only corroborate a birth year already carried by linked assertions, while the disproving evidence (a_003/a_004: Thomas of Ballingarry buried 1842, linked to I2) is fully linked. The narrowed gate must NOT stop on a_002/a_006 (corroborating-only, hence advisory); it must proceed, write the `disproved` conclusion, and surface a_002/a_006 as an advisory note rather than a blocker. Before the narrowing this scenario hard-stopped at the gate and produced no proof_summary.

input.user_message: An online tree says Thomas Flynn of Ballingarry was Patrick's father. Write the conclusion on whether that holds up.

judge_context: This test targets the precondition gate specifically. a_002 (1850 census age) and a_006 (1908 death-certificate age) have no person_evidence link, but both only corroborate Patrick's ~1845 birth year, which is already carried by linked assertions. The correct behavior is to treat a_002/a_006 as advisory and PROCEED to write the conclusion.
A hard stop at the gate that refuses to conclude and defers to person-evidence is a FAIL for this test — the corroborating-only unlinked assertions must not block the conclusion.
It is acceptable (and good) for the skill to note a_002/a_006 as an advisory (e.g. 'these could also be linked but are not required to conclude'); such a note must not prevent the conclusion.
Should select the `disproved` tier: the evidence affirmatively refutes the claim — Thomas of Ballingarry was buried in 1842, Patrick was born ~1845 — it does not merely fail to support it.
Must NOT update tree.gedcomx.json — no ParentChild relationship is added; the real father is left as a separate future question.
narrative_markdown should cite the Ballingarry burial register and the birth-year evidence and stand alone.
